title: Publisher Confirms, Windows, and Ambiguity · Stakeholder Go Mission
summary: A regional emergency coordination network. They need Go publishers and independent subscriber responsibilities with compatible envelopes, bounded admission, return handling, confirmation windows, late-subscriber policy, and ambiguous-publication reconciliation. Planned artifact: Deliver Emergency Multi-Subscriber Event Backbone with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, c…
objectives: Give each independent subscriber responsibility its own queue so every responsibility can receive and acknowledge its copy; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Test producer and consumer compatibility for additive, optional, renamed, split, semantic, and removal changes across an overlap window; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Pause admission after false, coordinate a bounded drain waiter, handle close and abort, and avoid lost wakeups or listener leaks; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility
steps.instruction:
project case — A project Go messaging team handles case 3685 during canary Go consumer decoding old and new envelopes. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound outstanding confirms and preserve ambiguity when a channel closes before proof; prove cleanup under a single deadline and cancellation cause; then raise a broker resource alarm and halt new admission. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Give each independent subscriber responsibility its own queue so every responsibility can receive and acknowledge its copy; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

What must be established before implementation?
